chunks.title: Installation
chunks.text: In the code, this is the new python notebook which we have created.

chunks.title: Installation
chunks.text: Let's rename it to numpy-tutorial.ipynb

chunks.title: Installation
chunks.text: and we are going to cover our numpy in this.

chunks.title: Installation
chunks.text: Now, because we are using anaconda, numpy comes installed by default.

chunks.title: Installation
chunks.text: We don't have to install it separately.

chunks.title: Installation
chunks.text: And we can import it directly as np.

chunks.title: Installation
chunks.text: Now, numpy is a package which has an industry standard that we import as np.

chunks.title: Installation
chunks.text: When we are importing numpy as a package, we will call it as np in the entire code.

chunks.title: Installation
chunks.text: Here, this name is np.

chunks.title: Installation
chunks.text: It could be anything.

chunks.title: Installation
chunks.text: It could also be prime.

chunks.title: Installation
chunks.text: It could also be shraddha.

chunks.title: Installation
chunks.text: It could be any name.

chunks.title: Installation
chunks.text: But the industry standard is that we import it as np.

chunks.title: Installation
chunks.text: From today, we will call our numpy as np.

chunks.title: Installation
chunks.text: And all the operations will be called as np.np.sum.

chunks.title: Installation
chunks.text: We can refer it like this.

chunks.title: Installation
chunks.text: So, this is how we import numpy.

chunks.title: Installation
chunks.text: If we are not using anaconda by chance,

chunks.title: Installation
chunks.text: and if we have installed python with pip,

chunks.title: Installation
chunks.text: then we have to install numpy with the command pip install numpy.

chunks.title: Installation
chunks.text: So, we can write the command like this.

chunks.title: Installation
chunks.text: But we don't have to write it because we have already completed the installation of anaconda.

chunks.title: Installation
chunks.text: In fact, if numpy is not installed for any reason,

chunks.title: Installation
chunks.text: then we can install it with pip install numpy.

chunks.title: Installation
chunks.text: You can directly run this command with an exclamation.

chunks.title: Installation
chunks.text: Or I can write 3 here and run this command.

chunks.title: Installation
chunks.text: So, here it has started the installation for numpy.

chunks.title: Installation
chunks.text: So, numpy is installed here.

chunks.title: Installation
chunks.text: Now, if I want, I can delete it.

chunks.title: Installation
chunks.text: And I can actually do import numpy as np.

chunks.title: Installation
chunks.text: And I will be able to use it.

chunks.title: Installation
chunks.text: Now, we have imported numpy.

chunks.title: Installation
chunks.text: How exactly do we create numpy arrays?

chunks.title: Installation
chunks.text: The easiest way is to create a python list.

chunks.title: Installation
chunks.text: So, if we want, we can create a python list.

chunks.title: Installation
chunks.text: It is listed in our python.

chunks.title: Installation
chunks.text: But if we want, we can convert it into a numpy array.

chunks.title: Installation
chunks.text: Basically, to convert it into a numpy array,

chunks.title: Installation
chunks.text: we have a simple function called np.array.

chunks.title: Installation
chunks.text: In which we pass our list and we get a numpy array.

chunks.title: Installation
chunks.text: So, in this way, I can create a numpy array and I can also print it.

chunks.title: Installation
chunks.text: So, we have created such an array.

chunks.title: Installation
chunks.text: And this is different from a list.

chunks.title: Installation
chunks.text: If we want, we can print the type of this array.

chunks.title: Installation
chunks.text: And the type is going to be numpy.ndarray.

chunks.title: Installation
chunks.text: The meaning of nd here is n-dimensional.

chunks.title: Installation
chunks.text: All the lists we have dealt with so far, they are all one-dimensional lists.

chunks.title: Installation
chunks.text: But whenever we talk about numpy arrays, they are 1D, 2D, 3D.

chunks.title: Installation
chunks.text: In fact, we can create them with n dimensions.

chunks.title: Installation
chunks.text: In this lecture, we are going to see in detail

chunks.title: Installation
chunks.text: what exactly the meaning of 2D array and 3D array is.

chunks.title: Installation
chunks.text: But for now, we can just remember the term that these are n-dimensional arrays.

chunks.title: Installation
chunks.text: Which can have as many dimensions as n dimensions.

text: In the code, this is the new python notebook which we have created. Let's rename it to numpy-tutorial.ipynb and we are going to cover our numpy in this. Now, because we are using anaconda, numpy comes installed by default. We don't have to install it separately. And we can import it directly as np. Now, numpy is a package which has an industry standard that we import as np. When we are importing numpy as a package, we will call it as np in the entire code. Here, this name is np. It could be anything. It could also be prime. It could also be shraddha. It could be any name. But the industry standard is that we import it as np. From today, we will call our numpy as np. And all the operations will be called as np.np.sum. We can refer it like this. So, this is how we import numpy. If we are not using anaconda by chance, and if we have installed python with pip, then we have to install numpy with the command pip install numpy. So, we can write the command like this. But we don't have to write it because we have already completed the installation of anaconda. In fact, if numpy is not installed for any reason, then we can install it with pip install numpy. You can directly run this command with an exclamation. Or I can write 3 here and run this command. So, here it has started the installation for numpy. So, numpy is installed here. Now, if I want, I can delete it. And I can actually do import numpy as np. And I will be able to use it. Now, we have imported numpy. How exactly do we create numpy arrays? The easiest way is to create a python list. So, if we want, we can create a python list. It is listed in our python. But if we want, we can convert it into a numpy array. Basically, to convert it into a numpy array, we have a simple function called np.array. In which we pass our list and we get a numpy array. So, in this way, I can create a numpy array and I can also print it. So, we have created such an array. And this is different from a list. If we want, we can print the type of this array. And the type is going to be numpy.ndarray. The meaning of nd here is n-dimensional. All the lists we have dealt with so far, they are all one-dimensional lists. But whenever we talk about numpy arrays, they are 1D, 2D, 3D. In fact, we can create them with n dimensions. In this lecture, we are going to see in detail what exactly the meaning of 2D array and 3D array is. But for now, we can just remember the term that these are n-dimensional arrays. Which can have as many dimensions as n dimensions.
